title: Early Head Start * Migrants - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > Early Head Start * Migrants 24 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Migrants Native American Community FIND CHILDCARE OREGON FIND CHILD CARE OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps families locate child care through referrals and provides resources to help make informed decisions about child care needs. 211info is available to provide families referrals to support their child care search, or families can conduct their own search on the website. Website Eligibility: Parents/caregivers of children up to age 18 Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-11pm, Saturday-Sunday 8am-8pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details OREGON CHILD DEVELOPMENT COALITION ANDERSON CENTER MIGRANT SEASONAL HEAD START Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Migrant and Seasonal Head Start programs provide education, health, nutrition, social services, and additional support, including developmental screening and is available for migrant children while their parents work in the fields. The program helps preschoolers develop the language, reading, and math skills they need to be successful in school. Parental involvement is emphasized. Services for parents and pregnant women include parenting classes, family support, and leadership opportunities. Wrap-around child care may also be available. 2727 SE Anderson Road Gresham, OR 97080 Eligibility: Migrant and Seasonal Head Start must be 3-5 years old, and have parents who are seasonal or migrant workers that have moved from another geographical location within the last 2 years. Family income must be 100% federal poverty level and come primarily from agricultural labor. Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 663-0896 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON CHILD DEVELOPMENT COALITION BOARDMAN CENTER MIGRANT SEASONAL HEAD START Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Migrant and Seasonal Head Start programs provide education, health, nutrition, social services, and additional support, including developmental screening and is available for migrant children while their parents work in the fields.
